danger_signals.visual_form: Countdown timer (Genesis version); implicit passage in Super NES version
danger_signals.meaning: Elsie-Dee approaches to kill the player; game over state imminent
Wolverine taking damage; death risk if depleted completely
Combat threat; Super NES version requires destruction before progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Twisted, distorted level aesthetics (implied by narrative)
danger_signals.meaning: Psychological danger; Wolverine trapped in toxic nightmare state
Approaching final boss confrontation; deeper danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Health percentage bar increasing over time without damage
safety_signals.meaning: Wolverine's healing factor active; safe period to regenerate

safety_signals.visual_form: Level progression gates/doors opening after enemy destruction or puzzle completion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone reached; time limit resets (Genesis version)

safety_signals.visual_form: Final level environment; familiar X-Men base
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; mission completion and homecoming

safety_signals.visual_form: Ability to record progress via password
safety_signals.meaning: Game state preservation; player agency and safety net

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: X-Mansion location; Professor X, Storm character presence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: X-Mansion as final sanctuary; character dialogue from allied heroes

faction_indicators.faction: The Hand / Great Beast (primary antagonist faction)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Hand organization; Leader: Great Beast
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hand forces as generic enemies; Geist's forces (enemy variant)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Inner Circle mansion in Tokyo; catacombs beneath mansion as faction stronghold

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Weapon X facility (Genesis version location); Professor Thornton character
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Weapon X facility enemies; experimental environment aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Weapon X facility level (Genesis version); laboratory-style architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Inner Circle mansion location; Shinobi Shaw as leader contact
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Shaw's forces; minions fighting Wolverine through mansion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tokyo-based mansion with catacombs; urban Japanese setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple antagonist encounters across levels; enemy variety increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies pursue Wolverine through multiple stages; escalating difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Solo traversal through levels without allied support visuals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Professor X and Storm appear but Wolverine rejects assistance; lonely journey aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distorted, nightmare-like level design during Cyber boss fight
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wolverine must counter-poison enemy; inverted danger mechanic (toxin as weapon)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from Inner Circle mansion to catacombs to final boss chamber
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shaw flees; new final antagonist (Great Beast) revealed; escalation of threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer visible (Genesis); implicit level timeout (Super NES)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elsie-Dee hunting mechanism forces pacing; failure if time expires

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to X-Mansion; greeting from X-Men allies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Peaceful ending state; character reunions signal victory and narrative closure

environmental_storytelling.example: Canada → Japan → Tokyo Inner Circle mansion → catacombs reveals plot escalation; geography mirrors story arc from mystery to conspiracy to final revelation
Lady Deathstrike informs Wolverine of the contract; Cyber traps him in nightmare revealing toxin connection; Shaw flees revealing greater mastermind
Weapon X facility (Genesis) conveys experimental/scientific threat; Inner Circle mansion in Tokyo conveys wealth and organization; catacombs convey ancient, hidden power
Geist's forces in early levels; Cyber as mid-game psychological test; Great Beast at deepest catacombs signals ultimate threat tier
Countdown timer mechanic (especially Genesis) embeds temporal pressure into level design; Elsie-Dee hunting presence makes time a visible antagonist
Professor X's warnings signal danger ahead; Storm's appearance marks stage progression; Shaw's presence indicates contract still active

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can climb walls/ceilings (Super NES); perform multi-hit combos (Genesis); active exploration
Health bar visible and reduced; movement may be slower; healing passive over time
Must use enemy toxin against them; inverted combat mechanic; altered level perception
Must hurry through level; cannot linger; forced progression pace
Pursues Wolverine if time expires; instant kill mechanic; visible timer countdown (Genesis)
Require destruction (Super NES) or avoidance (Genesis); attack on sight; indicate level theme

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss characters (Lady Deathstrike, Cyber, Shaw, Great Beast)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Multi-phase combat; exposition delivery; level-ending confrontation; escalating threat tier
Offer help/warning; Wolverine refuses (Genesis narrative choice); dialogue-only presence; signal moral alignment

notes: Wolverine: Adamantium Rage uses early recharging health system as core manifestation of Wolverine's healing factor—this is both gameplay mechanic and world-state communication. The time-limit mechanic (Elsie-Dee hunting) is unique danger signal embedding temporal pressure as antagonist. Two versions (SNES vs Genesis) have different visual/behavioral implementations but similar narrative world-state progression. Catacombs location serves dual purpose: environmental storytelling (ancient power) and narrative climax (Great Beast reveal). Political situation is implicit: Inner Circle contract plots against Wolverine, Weapon X past haunts him, Hand organization controls Tokyo base. Faction identity relays through character encounters and location control rather than explicit visual markers (no faction colors/symbols documented).
